task_completion.description: Load the 1978 influenza dataset into GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet view or Algebra view in GeoGebra shows a list of data points loaded from flu_1978.csv.

task_completion.description: Input the dynamic SIR model differential equations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Algebra view shows the SIR differential equations (S', I', R') or an ODE solver command incorporating the equations with N=763.

task_completion.description: Apply the correct initial conditions to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Algebra view shows the initial conditions S(0)=762, I(0)=1, and R(0)=0 applied to the ODE solver or equations.

task_completion.description: Create variables or sliders for the transmission and recovery rates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Algebra view shows variables or sliders created for beta (transmission rate) and gamma (recovery rate).

task_completion.description: Fit the infected curve to the observed data by adjusting beta and gamma.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The infected curve (I) visually aligns with the data points on the graph. The Algebra view shows beta is between 1.41 and 1.91, and gamma is between 0.37 and 0.51.

task_completion.description: Ensure the infected curve is visually distinct from the data points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphics view displays both discrete data points (e.g., a scatter plot) and a continuous curve for the infected population, clearly distinguishable from one another.
GeoGebra window title or a save dialog indicates the file is named 'sir_analysis.ggb'.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GeoGebra application interface (Algebra, Graphics, Spreadsheet views), not just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually set up the ODEs rather than hardcoding a static curve.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view contains ODE commands (like SolveODE or NDSolve) or explicit differential equations linking S, I, and R, rather than a standalone hardcoded function.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual dataset for calibration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data points visible in the Graphics or Spreadsheet view follow an epidemic curve shape (rising to a peak and falling), confirming the CSV was loaded.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target parameters for the SIR model fit: Beta should be approximately 1.66 (acceptable range: 1.41 to 1.91) and Gamma should be approximately 0.44 (acceptable range: 0.37 to 0.51). Population N is 763. The expected save path is ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/sir_analysis.ggb.
